title: LogScanResult
description: Output contract of `wrg-devguard scan-logs` (v0.2.0+). Frozen — adapters and consumers (CC log_viewer, CI integrations) rely on this shape. Additive-only changes via $defs; breaking changes require a major bump in tool_version.

schema_version.description: Frozen at 1 for the v0.2.0 line. Bump to 2 only on breaking change.

tool_version.description: Semver of wrg-devguard that produced this report (e.g., "0.2.0").

scanned_at.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the scan was performed.

source.description: Where the input came from. v0.2.0 supports `manual` only; `ci` and `cc-endpoint` reserved for v0.3.0.

path.description: Absolute or relative path of the scanned file. Empty string allowed when input was piped via stdin (path is then "<stdin>").

size_bytes.description: Total bytes read from the input.

lines.description: Total newline-terminated lines scanned. A trailing partial line counts as 1.

sha256.description: Optional SHA-256 of the input bytes. Useful for de-duplication across re-scans.

findings.description: Per-finding records. Order: ascending by (line_no, span[0]). Empty array means a clean scan.

by_severity.description: Counts per severity. Keys with zero may be omitted.

by_category.description: Counts per category (open enum, mirrors Finding.category).

runtime_ms.description: Optional wall-clock runtime of the scan, in milliseconds.

Severity.description: Default severity. UI consumers (e.g., CC log_viewer) decide presentation. `--strict` mode in the CLI may promote `info` → `low` for exit-code purposes; the JSON itself records the unpromoted severity.

Category.description: Open enum. Additive-only — v0.3.0 added `pii_name` (NAME-001 detector). Consumers must accept unknown values gracefully (treat as a generic finding, do not crash).

pattern_id.description: Stable identifier of the pattern that matched. Format: `<NAMESPACE>-<NNN>`. Examples: `AWS-001`, `EMAIL-001`, `JWT-001`. Never reused across pattern revisions; superseded patterns get a new ID.

line_no.description: 1-based line number in the input. Multi-line matches use the line of span[0].

span.description: [start, end) character offsets within the line. Half-open interval, like Python slicing.

redacted_excerpt.description: Middle-masked match. Producers MUST redact: keep ≤6 leading and ≤6 trailing characters of the matched span; replace the middle with `*` (>=4 stars). Never emit the raw value. CC log_viewer renders this verbatim.

rationale.description: Short human explanation of why this matched. Used in CC log_viewer's row-expand. Avoid quoting the raw value.

fp_suppression.description: Optional. If the producer downgraded severity due to a known false-positive heuristic (e.g., RFC-1918 IP, test-file path, example.com email), name the rule here. Empty/absent means no suppression applied.

findings.rationale: AWS access key ID format match (AKIA prefix + 16 uppercase alphanumerics).
